steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question says P6 gave equal ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. The graph shows the correlation between model-predicted ratings and actual ratings is higher for abstract (0.42) than for cubist (0.27). We need to determine what the model must have predicted about P6.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — P6's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another.: If P6's actual ratings were equal but the correlations differ by style, the model's predictions must have differed — otherwise the correlations would match. This is directly supported. ✓
  • Choice B — P6 would derive less aesthetic pleasure from abstract paintings than from cubist paintings.: Claims abstract < cubist, but the higher abstract correlation (0.42 vs 0.27) contradicts this direction. ✗
  • Choice C — P6's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would equal one another.: If the model predicted equal ratings, correlation patterns across styles should be similar — they're not. ✗
  • Choice D — P6 would derive more aesthetic pleasure from abstract paintings than from cubist paintings.: Asserts a specific direction the graph does not directly support for P6 individually. ✗
That matches Choice A — P6's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another..
